_meta.title: Hondata FlashPro Reference
_meta.description: Comprehensive structured reference for FlashPro sensors, parameters, calibration tables, and features extracted from official Hondata documentation.

sensors._description: All datalog sensors available via FlashPro datalogging. These values are captured in each datalog frame.

RPM.name: RPM
RPM.description: Engine speed in revolutions per minute, calculated from the crank position sensor. Extremely accurate; factory tachometers typically read 0-5% over.
tips: FlashPro RPM is more accurate than the dash tachometer
Most tachometers read 0-5% high

VSS.name: VSS
VSS.description: Vehicle ground speed calculated from the VSS sensor located on the transmission. Measures driven wheel speed; wheel slip and spin are not compensated for.
tips: Measures driven wheel speed only
Does not compensate for wheel slip or spin

Gear.name: Gear
Gear.description: Current gear as calculated by the ECU from vehicle speed and RPM.
tips: Calculated value, not a direct sensor reading
0 indicates neutral or clutch disengaged

MAP.name: MAP
MAP.description: Air pressure inside the intake manifold measured after the throttle plate. On forced induction vehicles the MAP sensor should read manifold pressure after the supercharger/turbocharger.
tips: Stock MAP sensor reads up to ~1.78 bar (~11.5 psi boost)
Hondata 4 bar MAP sensor reads up to ~40 psi boost
Do not set boost cut over 1.75 bar with stock MAP sensor

TPedal.name: TPedal
TPedal.description: Throttle pedal position measured as percentage of full throttle (0-100%).

TPlate.name: TPlate
TPlate.description: Throttle plate (butterfly valve) position measured as percentage of fully open (0-100%). The ECU maps throttle pedal to plate position; the plate opens less at low RPM for smoothness.

ECT.name: ECT
ECT.description: Engine coolant temperature measured in the cylinder head.
tips: Normal operating temp ~185-210°F (85-99°C)
Stock thermostat opens at 185°F (85°C), fully open at 195°F (90°C)

IAT.name: IAT
IAT.description: Temperature of intake air. For naturally aspirated vehicles, measured in the intake pipe by the AFM. For turbo vehicles, measured in the intake manifold.
tips: Location differs between NA and turbo vehicles
Affects ignition and fuel compensation tables

IGN.name: IGN
IGN.description: Ignition timing measured in degrees before top dead center (BTDC). This is the actual ignition timing including all compensations.
tips: At idle, ECU uses ignition to control idle speed; timing ranges from -10 to +15 degrees
Excessive advance damages the engine
Actual timing may differ from base table due to knock control, temperature compensation, etc.

INJ.name: INJ
INJ.description: Injector pulse width duration for #1 injector, usually measured in milliseconds.

DUTY.name: DUTY
DUTY.description: Injector duty cycle as a percentage of available time. High duty cycle indicates the injectors are approaching their flow limit.
tips: Over 85% duty cycle indicates injectors are near capacity
Consider larger injectors if regularly above 80%

AF.name: AF
AF.description: Air/fuel ratio calculated from the stock lambda sensor.
tips: Stoichiometric is 14.7:1 for gasoline
Stock wideband reads 10.7:1 to 20:1 but progressively reads richer as EGT increases
For forced induction, use aftermarket wideband for accurate WOT readings

AFM.name: AFM
AFM.description: Air flow meter reading converted to mass flow. The AFM measures airflow into the engine and produces a voltage which the ECU converts to grams per second.

AFM.v.name: AFM.v
AFM.v.description: Raw voltage output from the air flow meter before conversion to mass flow units.

BAT.name: BAT
BAT.description: Battery voltage as measured by the ECU.
tips: Normal running voltage is 13.5-14.5V
Below 12V may indicate charging system issues

CAM.name: CAM
CAM.description: Actual intake camshaft position measured in degrees of crankshaft rotation from the base (fully retarded) position. Locked at zero for the first 10 seconds after startup.
tips: Locked at 0 degrees for 10 seconds after startup
May lag CAMCMD by 0.1-0.5 seconds
Normal range 0-50 degrees depending on calibration

CAMCMD.name: CAMCMD
CAMCMD.description: Desired (target) intake camshaft position based on the cam advance table and engine conditions. The actual CAM position may lag this value by 0.1-0.5 seconds due to the closed-loop VTC mechanism.
tips: Compare with CAM to verify VTC is tracking properly
Large difference between CAMCMD and CAM may indicate VTC problems

VTS.name: VTS
VTS.description: Shows when VTEC has engaged the high speed cam. Binary indicator.
tips: 1 = high speed cam engaged
0 = low speed cam active

S.TRIM.name: S.TRIM
S.TRIM.description: Short term fuel trim used in closed loop to keep the air/fuel ratio near stoichiometric for optimal catalytic converter operation. Positive values mean the ECU is adding fuel; negative values mean it is removing fuel.
tips: Normal range is -10% to +10%
Outside this range indicates fuel maps need part-throttle tuning
Often goes very high during fuel overrun conditions while shifting

L.TRIM.name: L.TRIM
L.TRIM.description: Long term fuel trim based on the trend of short term fuel trim. Represents a learned adjustment the ECU applies over time.
tips: Normal range is -5% to +5%
Large values indicate a consistent fueling error that should be corrected in the calibration

TRIM.name: TRIM
TRIM.description: Combined long term and short term fuel trim value.

Fuel Status.name: Fuel Status
Fuel Status.description: Shows if the ECU is running in open loop or closed loop mode.
tips: Status 2 (closed loop) is normal at part throttle
Status 4 at WOT is normal behavior

K.Count.name: K.Count
K.Count.description: Knock count per cylinder. Incremented each time the ECU determines a knock-type event from the knock sensor. Recorded individually for each cylinder.
tips: Not affected by knock sensitivity table changes
Supercharger noise and exhaust headers are common causes of false knock
Monitor per-cylinder to identify problem areas

K.Level.name: K.Level
K.Level.description: Knock level as detected by the knock sensor, measured in percentage.

K.Retard.name: K.Retard
K.Retard.description: Amount of ignition timing retard applied by the knock control system, measured in degrees.
tips: 0 degrees means no knock retard is being applied
Persistent retard indicates the engine is knocking and needs attention

K.Control.name: K.Control
K.Control.description: Knock control value in percent. Represents the ECU's estimate of fuel octane quality. 0% means the ECU considers the fuel high octane; higher values mean lower estimated octane, applying more retard.
tips: Starts high after ECU reflash and slowly comes down during normal driving
Hold steady RPM on dyno to reduce K.Control faster
50% means halfway between high and low octane knock limits

Ign.Limit.name: Ign.Limit
Ign.Limit.description: The current knock ignition limit in degrees. Maximum timing the ECU will allow based on knock control tables.

PA.name: PA
PA.description: Atmospheric (barometric) air pressure measured internally by the ECU.
tips: Sea level is approximately 101.3 kPa
Lower at higher altitudes

Eco.name: Eco
Eco.description: Fuel economy as calculated by the ECU. Requires correct injector size settings in fuel parameters for accuracy.

Fuel Used.name: Fuel Used
Fuel Used.description: Fuel consumed during this trip, calculated by the ECU from injector duration and flow. Requires correct injector size settings.

calibration_tables._description: Tuning tables available in FlashPro calibrations. Tables are organized by category.

fuel_tables_low_cam.name: Fuel Tables (Low Cam)
fuel_tables_low_cam.description: Volumetric efficiency tables for the low speed cam when using speed-density (MAP-based) fueling. Not used by AFM-based systems.

fuel_tables_high_cam.name: Fuel Tables (High Cam)
fuel_tables_high_cam.description: Volumetric efficiency tables for the high speed cam when using speed-density (MAP-based) fueling.

wot_lambda_low_cam.name: WOT Lambda Adjustment (Low Cam)
wot_lambda_low_cam.description: Wide open throttle lambda/AFR target for the low speed cam. Applied when ECU determines WOT conditions. Based on stoichiometric fueling assumption.
tips: Do not use values above 12.50 AFR or 0.85 lambda, otherwise ECU will always run closed loop

wot_lambda_high_cam.name: WOT Lambda Adjustment (High Cam)
wot_lambda_high_cam.description: Wide open throttle lambda/AFR target for the high speed cam.

afm_fuel.name: AFM Fuel
afm_fuel.description: Converts from air mass per cylinder to injector duration. Used on AFM-based vehicles.

individual_cylinder_fuel_trim.name: Individual Cylinder Fuel Trim
individual_cylinder_fuel_trim.description: Per-cylinder fuel trim to compensate for flow differences between injectors. Only adjust with individual cylinder lambda probes.

fuel_iat_compensation.name: Fuel IAT Compensation
fuel_iat_compensation.description: Three tables adjusting fuel by intake air temperature at low, medium, and high airflow rates. Positive values add fuel; negative values reduce fuel.

fuel_ect_compensation.name: Fuel ECT Compensation
fuel_ect_compensation.description: Two tables adjusting fuel by coolant temperature - one for low load (<40 kPa) and one for high load. Positive adds fuel; negative reduces fuel.

cranking_fuel_iat.name: Cranking Fuel IAT Compensation
cranking_fuel_iat.description: Adjusts cranking (starting) fuel by intake air temperature.

cranking_fuel_ect.name: Cranking Fuel ECT Compensation
cranking_fuel_ect.description: Adjusts cranking (starting) fuel by coolant temperature.

ignition_tables_low_cam.name: Ignition Tables (Low Cam)
ignition_tables_low_cam.description: Base ignition timing (MBT) for the low speed cam. Additional compensations (knock, temperature, etc.) are applied on top of these values.

ignition_tables_high_cam.name: Ignition Tables (High Cam)
ignition_tables_high_cam.description: Base ignition timing (MBT) for the high speed cam.

egr_ignition_tables.name: EGR Ignition Tables
egr_ignition_tables.description: Affects partial throttle ignition timing when EGR is between 12-25%. Indexed by RPM and EGR percentage.

individual_cylinder_ignition_trim.name: Individual Cylinder Ignition Trim
individual_cylinder_ignition_trim.description: Per-cylinder ignition timing trim. Only adjust using exhaust gas temperature gauge data.

ignition_iat_compensation.name: Ignition IAT Compensation
ignition_iat_compensation.description: Two tables compensating ignition timing by intake air temperature - low load and medium/high load (crossover at ~40 kPa). Positive values retard; negative values advance.

ignition_ect_cold.name: Ignition ECT Compensation (Cold)
ignition_ect_cold.description: Retards ignition when coolant is below normal operating temperature. Two tables for low and high load.

ignition_ect_hot.name: Ignition ECT Compensation (Hot)
ignition_ect_hot.description: Retards ignition when coolant is above normal (overheating). Two tables for low and high load.

ignition_gear_compensation.name: Ignition Gear Compensation
ignition_gear_compensation.description: Adjusts ignition timing by gear. Positive values advance, negative values retard. Applies to all load/rpm points.

cranking_ignition.name: Cranking Ignition
cranking_ignition.description: Base ignition timing during engine cranking, indexed by coolant temperature.

throttle_tip_in_retard.name: Throttle Tip-In Ignition Retard
throttle_tip_in_retard.description: Ignition retard upon throttle tip-in, with RPM, throttle, and gear compensation sub-tables.

cam_angle_low_cam.name: Cam Angle Table (Low Cam)
cam_angle_low_cam.description: Target intake cam advance angle for the low speed cam, indexed by RPM and MAP. Values in crank degrees from base position.
tips: Set to 0 at idle and low RPM
15-30 degrees at cruising conditions
0.1 seconds per 10 degrees rotation delay

cam_angle_high_cam.name: Cam Angle Table (High Cam)
cam_angle_high_cam.description: Target intake cam advance angle for the high speed cam.

knock_ignition_limit_low_cam.name: Knock Ignition Limit (Low Cam)
knock_ignition_limit_low_cam.description: Maximum ignition advance before knock is likely, relative to the MBT ignition table. Negative values mean the knock limit is lower than MBT timing.
tips: Caution: increasing negative values toward zero advances timing
For forced induction on pump fuel, use 0 or less in boost columns

knock_ignition_limit_high_cam.name: Knock Ignition Limit (High Cam)
knock_ignition_limit_high_cam.description: Knock ignition limit table for the high speed cam.

knock_sensitivity.name: Knock Sensitivity
knock_sensitivity.description: Threshold for knock sensor noise before ECU determines knock is occurring. Lower values = more sensitive.
tips: Increase values for noisy cams/headers to reduce false knock
Decrease values for better knock detection with forced induction

knock_retard.name: Knock Retard Tables
knock_retard.description: Amount of retard between high-octane and low-octane knock limits. Actual retard depends on K.Control value. Only retards when knock limit < MBT timing.
tips: Use 4-10 degrees for aftermarket tuning
Lower columns (6 and below) have little effect

knock_air_limit.name: Knock Air Limit
knock_air_limit.description: Cylinder filling limit (mg) based on knock retard. Can be increased without adversely affecting K.Control. Helps make boost levels more consistent as knock retard varies.

vtec_rpm_window.name: VTEC RPM Window
vtec_rpm_window.description: Minimum and maximum RPM for VTEC activation.
tips: Do not set below 2500 rpm on twin cam, 1800 on single cam, 1000 on R18/R20
Do not set above 6500 rpm to prevent valve train damage
Stock engines typically 5500-5700 rpm
Intake & exhaust mods: 4500-4800 rpm
Supercharged: 3000-3200 rpm

vtec_window_pressure.name: VTEC Window Pressure
vtec_window_pressure.description: Minimum MAP for VTEC activation vs RPM. Prevents cam switch at light throttle for better fuel economy and driveability.
tips: Set 80 kPa at lower RPM, 40 kPa at upper RPM for NA
Set 110-120 kPa at lower RPM for forced induction

parameters._description: All tunable parameters organized by subcategory.

injector_size.name: Injector Size
injector_size.description: Current injector flow rate. Changing this automatically compensates main fuel tables, cranking fuel, and closed loop operation.
tips: Flow is nominally at 3 bar (43.5 psi)
Changing size auto-compensates fuel tables

fuel_pressure.name: Fuel Pressure
fuel_pressure.description: Compensates for fuel pressure if different from stock. Includes test pressure values for both stock and current injectors.

fuel_trim_compensation.name: Fuel Trim Compensation
fuel_trim_compensation.description: Manual compensation of main fuel tables, AFM tables, and cranking fuel. Normally not needed if injector size is set correctly.

injector_opening_time.name: Injector Opening Time (Dead Time)
injector_opening_time.description: Injector latency/dead time compensation. Manufacturer provides these values for aftermarket injectors.

overrun_fuel_cut_delay.name: Overrun Fuel Cut Delay
overrun_fuel_cut_delay.description: Delay between overrun conditions being met (throttle closed, RPM >1800) and injectors shutting off. Long delay gives rev hang; short delay makes the vehicle jerky.
tips: For 6-speed vehicles, 5th gear setting is used for both 5th and 6th gears

fuel_table_type.name: Fuel Table Type
fuel_table_type.description: For some AFM calibrations, allows using MAP (VE) lookup tables instead of AFM flow. Can also blend AFM at low pressure and MAP at higher pressures.

fuel_economy.name: Fuel Economy Parameters
fuel_economy.description: Injector flow reference values used by ECU and dash for fuel economy calculations.

cold_retard.name: Cold Start Ignition Retard
cold_retard.description: When cold, ECU retards ignition to heat catalytic converter faster. Can be disabled for race vehicles without a catalyst.

iat_retard_target.name: IAT Retard Application
iat_retard_target.description: Controls whether IAT-based ignition retard is applied to the knock ignition limit tables or directly to ignition advance.
tips: Only one option should be checked at a time

idle_speed_normal.name: Idle Speed (Normal)
idle_speed_normal.description: Target idle speed indexed by coolant temperature. Used after the after-start period (20-60 seconds).
tips: With larger injectors, increase idle speed slightly for better quality idle

idle_speed_after_start.name: Idle Speed (After Start)
idle_speed_after_start.description: Elevated idle speed used immediately after engine start. Returns to normal idle speed after 20-60 seconds.

idle_torque_reserve.name: Idle Torque Reserve
idle_torque_reserve.description: Percentage of torque held in reserve at idle for stability, created by retarding timing. Target ~0 degrees average ignition at idle.
tips: Too low = ECU may increase idle speed to compensate
Too high = ignition overly retarded

vtec_min_coolant_temp.name: VTEC Minimum Coolant Temperature
vtec_min_coolant_temp.description: Minimum water temperature before VTEC will activate.

vtec_engage_speed.name: VTEC Engage Minimum Speed
vtec_engage_speed.description: Minimum vehicle speed for VTEC activation.

vtec_disengage_speed.name: VTEC Disengage Speed
vtec_disengage_speed.description: Vehicle speed below which VTEC disengages. Should always be lower than engage speed.

vtc_decel_zero.name: Zero VTC Upon Decel Fuel Cut
vtc_decel_zero.description: Default behavior returns cam to 0 degrees when throttle is released. Uncheck for smoother gear shifts.
tips: Recommended to disable for smoother shifting
Set decel angle or use VTC tables instead

vtc_decel_angle.name: VTC Decel Fuel Cut Angle
vtc_decel_angle.description: Fixed cam angle to use during deceleration instead of returning to zero. Typically 20-30 degrees works well.

vtc_use_tables_decel.name: Use VTC Tables Upon Decel Fuel Cut
vtc_use_tables_decel.description: Uses normal VTC table lookup during deceleration for the smoothest shifting.
tips: Recommended: disable Zero VTC and enable this
Ensure idle area of VTC tables is set to 0 degrees

vtc_pid.name: VTC Closed Loop PID
vtc_pid.description: PID parameters for the VTC closed loop cam position control. Only change if needed; will not fix mechanical problems.

knock_sensor_enabled.name: Knock Sensor
knock_sensor_enabled.description: Enable/disable the knock sensor. If disabled, ECU uses default (conservative) advance tables resulting in considerable retard.
tips: Normally does not need to be disabled during tuning

knock_light_control.name: Knock Light Control
knock_light_control.description: When enabled, flashes the check engine light when knock occurs.

target_throttle_plate.name: Target Throttle Plate Table
target_throttle_plate.description: Maps throttle pedal position to throttle plate opening. Advanced table - incorrect settings could cause sudden acceleration.
target_throttle_plate.warning: Advanced table. Incorrect settings could result in the throttle not responding normally.

throttle_dampening.name: Throttle Dampening
throttle_dampening.description: Slows throttle opening in 1st gear while moving, which may cause a jerk when the throttle opens fully. Can be disabled.

overrun_throttle.name: Overrun Throttle Opening
overrun_throttle.description: Holds throttle plate slightly open during overrun (deceleration).

throttle_flow.name: Throttle Flow Table
throttle_flow.description: Air flow past the throttle body for a given throttle opening. Only needs adjustment for larger-than-stock throttle bodies.

throttle_tip_in_fuel.name: Throttle Tip-In Fuel
throttle_tip_in_fuel.description: Extra fuel added when throttle is opened quickly. Includes ECT and throttle position compensation sub-tables.

injector_overrun_cutoff.name: Injector Overrun Cutoff
injector_overrun_cutoff.description: Controls when fuel injectors are shut off during deceleration. Separate tables for cut/recover RPM and MAP thresholds for low and high speed cams.

overall_limiter.name: Overall Rev Limiter
overall_limiter.description: Maximum engine speed the ECU will allow under any conditions. Uses fuel cut method. Will not protect against misshift overrev.
tips: Set recovery 100-300 rpm lower than activation
Larger difference = slower, rougher limit

overrev_rpm.name: Overrev RPM
overrev_rpm.description: Engine speed where ECU generates a fault condition. Set ~200 rpm above overall rev limiter.

launch_limiter.name: Launch Limiter
launch_limiter.description: Active when vehicle is stationary for drivetrain protection. Fastest action when cut and recovery RPM are the same.

speed_limiter.name: Speed Limiter
speed_limiter.description: Maximum vehicle speed limiter. Set higher than vehicle top speed to effectively disable.

boost_cut.name: Boost Cut
boost_cut.description: Rev limits the engine if manifold pressure exceeds the specified limit. Active above 2000 RPM only. Protects against over-boost.
tips: Do not set over 1.75 bar with stock MAP sensor
Only active above 2000 RPM to ensure engine can always start

full_throttle_shift.name: Full Throttle Shift (Flat Shift)
full_throttle_shift.description: Allows flat shifting by cutting fuel when clutch is depressed at WOT. ECU detects clutch + wide open throttle and cuts injectors for gear change.
tips: Set RPM limit 2000-3000 rpm below shift RPM
Minimum throttle prevents activation during part throttle shifts

primary_o2_sensor.name: Primary Oxygen Sensor
primary_o2_sensor.description: Enable/disable the primary O2 sensor for closed loop operation. Recommended to keep enabled during tuning.

secondary_o2_sensor.name: Secondary Oxygen Sensor
secondary_o2_sensor.description: Enable/disable the secondary O2 sensor (catalyst monitor). Should be enabled unless running a race vehicle without a catalyst.

wot_determination_type.name: WOT Determination Type
wot_determination_type.description: Use MAP or TPS to determine when to switch from closed loop to open loop. MAP is more consistent; required for forced induction.

map_wot_determination.name: MAP WOT Determination
map_wot_determination.description: Manifold pressure threshold for WOT (open loop) determination by RPM.

closed_loop_target_lambda.name: Closed Loop Target Lambda
closed_loop_target_lambda.description: Target lambda/AFR in closed loop. Uses the richer of multiple tables based on temperature and load.
closed_loop_target_lambda.warning: Running leaner than stoichiometric increases EGT. Not recommended for vehicles with catalysts.

fuel_trim_min_max.name: Fuel Trim Min/Max
fuel_trim_min_max.description: Controls the allowable range of short term and long term fuel trim in closed loop.

laf_voltage_to_lambda.name: LAF Voltage to Lambda
laf_voltage_to_lambda.description: Conversion table from stock wideband oxygen sensor voltage to lambda (AFR).

immobilizer.name: Immobilizer
immobilizer.description: Cannot be disabled for security reasons.

vsa.name: VSA (Vehicle Stability Assistance)
vsa.description: Enable/disable VSA. Should be enabled for all vehicles that support it.

eps.name: EPS (Electric Power Steering)
eps.description: Enables engine idle-up when steering wheel is turned. Does not enable/disable the EPS unit itself.

starter_relay.name: Starter Relay
starter_relay.description: Enable/disable the starter motor relay. Only needed for push-button start vehicles.

radiator_fan.name: Radiator Fan
radiator_fan.description: Temperature thresholds for radiator fan on/off. 'On' should be ~3°C/5°F higher than 'off'. Uses second coolant temp sensor (radiator bottom) by default.
tips: Stock thermostat opens at 85°C/185°F, fully open at 90°C/195°F
Setting fan temps below thermostat open temp means fans always on

max_pressure_ratio.name: Turbocharger Maximum Pressure Ratio
max_pressure_ratio.description: Controls maximum turbocharger discharge air pressure based on ambient pressure. Indexed by RPM and PA.

scramble.name: Scramble Mode
scramble.description: Bypasses built-in protection limits to target maximum boost levels from the pressure ratio table. Toggled via Res+ cruise control button (or Sport button on 10th gen Si).
tips: Cruise control must be off, ECON must be off
Raise torque limit table when using scramble
2017 Civic Si may need P0299 error disabled

boost_by_gear.name: Boost By Gear
boost_by_gear.description: Maximum boost pressure by gear to aid traction. Controls maximum boost, not actual boost.

boost_control_enabled.name: Boost Control Enabled
boost_control_enabled.description: Enables the boost control solenoid output. For NA-to-turbo conversions using external solenoid.
boost_control_enabled.pin_info: ECU pin B2 (Civic Si) or B16 (S2000)

solenoid_configuration.name: Solenoid Configuration
solenoid_configuration.description: Normally open or normally closed solenoid type. Normally open (e.g., GM) gives max boost at 0% duty; FlashPro inverts the display.
tips: Set boost limit as failure results in maximum boost with normally open solenoids

activation_pressure.name: Activation Pressure
activation_pressure.description: Manifold pressure above which the boost control solenoid activates. Set slightly over atmospheric pressure.

solenoid_frequency.name: Solenoid Frequency
solenoid_frequency.description: PWM frequency for the boost control solenoid output. Affects boost level and maximum achievable boost.
tips: Most solenoids operate best from 10-60 Hz

control_method.name: Control Method
control_method.description: Fixed duty cycle for flat boost, or duty cycle by RPM & gear for per-gear boost control.

boost_iat_compensation.name: Boost IAT Compensation
boost_iat_compensation.description: Adjusts boost duty cycle based on intake air temperature. Cooler air = more boost pressure for same duty.

boost_tps_compensation.name: Boost TPS Compensation
boost_tps_compensation.description: Adjusts boost duty cycle based on throttle pedal position.

quick_spool.name: Quick Spool
quick_spool.description: Opens boost solenoid fully to build boost faster. Returns to normal duty once target pressure is reached.
tips: Set activation pressure to 1 psi boost
Set quick spool 1-2 psi below target boost
Set values below activation pressure to disable

max_boost_by_speed.name: Max Boost by Speed
max_boost_by_speed.description: Limits boost pressure at different vehicle speeds for safety.

boost_pid.name: Boost Control PID
boost_pid.description: PID parameters for closed-loop boost control. I and D Smoothing parameters are very sensitive.
boost_pid.warning: Only modify I and D Smoothing if necessary and you know what you are doing.

ethanol_input.name: Ethanol Input
ethanol_input.description: Input source for ethanol content signal. Requires flex fuel sensor and control unit outputting 0-5V.

ethanol_percentage.name: Ethanol Percentage Table
ethanol_percentage.description: Defines signal range from ethanol control unit when using ECT2 input. Most units: 0V = 0%, 5V = 100%.
tips: Zeitronix ECA: 0.34V = 0%, 4.97V = 100% (won't read below 15%)
Innovate ECF-1: 0.00V = 0%, 5.00V = 100% (won't read below 1.5%)
US pump gas has 0-10% ethanol (typically 10%)
E85 is 55-80% depending on location and time of year

ethanol_fuel_compensation.name: Ethanol Fuel Compensation
ethanol_fuel_compensation.description: Additional fuel delivery based on ethanol percentage.

ethanol_ignition_multiplier.name: Ethanol Ignition Multiplier
ethanol_ignition_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the ethanol ignition compensation table based on ethanol percentage.

ethanol_ignition_compensation.name: Ethanol Ignition Compensation
ethanol_ignition_compensation.description: Ignition advance added based on ethanol content. Final value = table value x ignition multiplier.

ethanol_wot_fuel.name: Ethanol WOT Fuel Compensation
ethanol_wot_fuel.description: Adjusts WOT fueling by ethanol content. DI vehicles may respond better to leaner WOT AFR at high ethanol.
tips: DI vehicles may make more power at 11.8:1 vs 11:1 with 85% ethanol

boost_by_ethanol.name: Boost by Ethanol Limit
boost_by_ethanol.description: Limits maximum boost based on ethanol content. Higher ethanol allows more boost but requires more fuel.

advanced_ethanol.name: Advanced Ethanol
advanced_ethanol.description: Disables simple 2D tables and enables 3D fuel and boost tables for more precise ethanol tuning.

adjustable_launch_rpm.name: Adjustable Launch RPM
adjustable_launch_rpm.description: Launch control RPM adjustable via cruise control switches without reflashing. Use resume/set to change in 100 RPM increments with clutch depressed.
tips: Clutch must be depressed and vehicle stationary
Cancel button shows current launch RPM on tachometer
Value retained until battery disconnect or reflash

launch_disengage_speed.name: Launch RPM Disengage Speed
launch_disengage_speed.description: Maximum vehicle speed for launch control fuel and ignition changes.

launch_min_rpm_retard.name: Launch Min RPM for Retard/Enrichment
launch_min_rpm_retard.description: Engine speed threshold where launch-specific fuel and ignition changes activate. Set below launch RPM. Set above rev limiter to disable.

launch_additional_fuel.name: Launch Additional Fuel
launch_additional_fuel.description: Extra fuel added when launch limiter is active.
launch_additional_fuel.warning: Builds significant exhaust heat. Do not use with catalytic converter. Only use for a few seconds.

launch_ignition_retard.name: Launch Ignition Retard
launch_ignition_retard.description: Ignition retard when launch limiter is active. Positive values retard timing.
launch_ignition_retard.warning: Builds significant exhaust heat. Do not use with catalytic converter.

torque_limit.name: Torque Limit
torque_limit.description: Overall torque limit as percentage (0-100%) for transmission protection.
tips: CVT transmissions damaged by power braking or torque above ~250 Nm
Raise when using scramble mode to prevent throttle closing

reference_torque.name: Reference Torque
reference_torque.description: Provided for Bosch torque model visualization. Do not change as it affects cruise control and other functions.

nominal_charge_reference_torque.name: Nominal Charge / Reference Torque Tables
nominal_charge_reference_torque.description: Bosch torque model tables. If one is altered, the other must be updated to maintain inverse lookup. Use built-in modifications only.
nominal_charge_reference_torque.warning: Incorrect changes cause abrupt/unpredictable operation and possible throttle control disabling.

minimum_efficiency.name: Minimum Efficiency
minimum_efficiency.description: Minimum efficiency from ignition, lambda etc. when calculating air charge.

afm_flow.name: AFM Flow Table
afm_flow.description: Conversion from AFM voltage to air flow. Advanced table normally requiring a flow bench. Can be tuned by analyzing fuel trims at different AFM voltages.
afm_flow.warning: Advanced table. Normally requires a flow bench.

map_sensor_type.name: MAP Sensor Type
map_sensor_type.description: Stock MAP sensor reads ~1.7 bar absolute (~10 psi boost). Hondata 4 bar MAP sensor reads ~40 psi boost. No retune needed when switching.

speed_adjustment.name: Speed Adjustment
speed_adjustment.description: Adjusts calculated and displayed vehicle speed. Positive increases speed; negative decreases. Needed when final drive or 3rd gear ratios are changed.

gear_ratios.name: Gear Ratios
gear_ratios.description: Transmission gear ratios for correct cruise control operation and speed calculation with non-stock gearing.

laptop_usb.name: Laptop Datalogging (USB)
laptop_usb.description: Datalog via USB connection to laptop running FlashProManager.

onboard.name: On Board Datalogging
onboard.description: Datalog to FlashPro internal memory. Up to 20 hours of storage.

bluetooth.name: Bluetooth Datalogging
bluetooth.description: Datalog to smartphone via Bluetooth using compatible apps.

features.frame_based: All sensors captured in each frame snapshot
features.autosave: Datalogs can be automatically saved when recording stops or ignition is turned off

graph_templates.description: Configurable graph templates for the Graph Window with 1-4 sub-graphs, each containing 1-4 sensors.

traction_control_datalog.description: TC hardware module has 20-second datalog at 100 Hz. Stores last 2 seconds automatically. Triggered manually or by speed/slip thresholds.

bluetooth._description: Bluetooth connectivity for FlashPro datalogging to mobile devices.
bluetooth.availability: All FlashPros manufactured after December 2013 (some from July 2013).

v2_1.name: Bluetooth V2.1 (BR/EDR)
v2_1.description: Classic Bluetooth. Works with Android without restriction. Apple requires special chip (jailbreak workaround available).

ble.name: Bluetooth 4.0 (Low Energy / BLE)
ble.description: Works with Apple products (iPhone 4S+, iPad 3rd gen+). Slower than V2.1. Tuning via Bluetooth disabled for BLE.

protocols.name: ELM327 emulation
protocols.use: Compatibility with existing OBDII smartphone apps

compatible_devices.apple: iPhone 4S+, iPod touch 3rd gen+, iPad 3rd gen+
compatible_devices.other: Any device supporting ELM327 protocol using RFCOMM

options.tuning_via_bluetooth: Allows datalogging and some settings changes over Bluetooth (uploading not supported)
options.alternative_pairing_mode: Shows FlashPro as a phone for head unit pairing (required for 2018+ Civics)
options.limit_access_by_address: Restrict connections to specific device addresses (most secure)

vehicle_pairing.description: For Hondata mobile app on vehicle head units, FlashPro may need to be paired as a phone.
steps: Connect to laptop and enable 'Alternative pairing mode' in FlashPro Bluetooth options
Unplug and re-plug FlashPro from USB
On vehicle radio, pair to FlashPro with matching PIN code

error_codes._description: OBDII diagnostics and error code management.

dtcs.description: Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) set by the ECU to indicate problems. Expressed as 4-digit codes starting with 'P' for powertrain.
dtcs.tip: Fix all error codes before tuning the vehicle.

readiness_codes.description: OBDII readiness codes indicate if various emissions components have been tested by the ECU.
readiness_codes.reset_conditions: Readiness codes reset to 'incomplete' when ECU is reprogrammed, DTCs are cleared, ECU is removed, or battery is disconnected.

setting_guide.catalyst: 5 miles of stop/go driving with steady cruise periods. Needs working secondary O2 and good fuel tuning.
setting_guide.evap: Cold start, stop/go warm-up, then 5-10 min highway speed with steady throttle.
setting_guide.oxygen_sensor: Warm engine, 3.5 miles driving including 5 seconds of closed-throttle decel.
setting_guide.cold_start_definition: ECT below 120°F, IAT and ECT within 7°F, minimum 6 hours engine off.

emissions_check.description: Simulates the emissions test used by most testing stations.

diagnostics.description: Shows DTCs from all CAN bus modules. Refresh searches for common modules; Shift+Refresh performs exhaustive search (~1 minute). Clear DTCs with engine off, then switch off ignition for 1 minute.

types: Pending (input out of range, not yet triggered)
tc_errors.error_counters: Accumulated wheel speed sensor pulses and errors per wheel. High error count indicates bad sensor or wiring.

traction_control._description: Hondata traction control system - available as hardware module or software-based using VSA wheel speed data.

hardware.description: Uses an interface module to the wheel speed sensors (sold separately as Hondata Traction Control).
hardware.setup_note: TC module must be plugged in while FlashProManager is running at least once before options appear.

parameters.input_pin: ECU input pin from TC module. ECT2 not recommended (too slow for effective TC).
parameters.voltage_offset: Should be zero unless wiring ground problem exists.
parameters.minimum_rpm: Minimum engine speed for TC operation. Set above idle, below expected wheel slip RPM.
parameters.ignition_retard_table: Amount of ignition retard by wheel slip percentage. Values interpolated between columns.
parameters.light_control: Flashes alternator light when TC is retarding ignition.

software.description: Uses wheel speed data from factory VSA unit. Not available for all vehicles. Covered by US Patents 9,371,792; 9,091,224; 9,254,748.
software.requirements: Do not disable VSA in FlashProManager. VSA can be disabled via dashboard button.
advantages: Uses 'fast torque' method (ignition retard) for instant power reduction and restoration
Parameters under user control vs fixed factory VSA
software.differences_from_factory_vsa: Factory VSA uses 'slow torque' (throttle closing, boost reduction). Hondata TC uses 'fast torque' (ignition retard) for instant response.

parameters.enabled: Enable software TC. Can be disabled while driving by switching on cruise control main.
parameters.minimum_speed: Minimum un-driven wheel speed before TC operates.
parameters.target_slip: Maximum allowed wheel slip between driven and un-driven wheels.
parameters.ignition_retard: Retard amount by slip percentage, interpolated between columns.
parameters.cornering_correction: Compensates for different front/rear wheel speeds during turning. Calculated from track and wheelbase.
parameters.launch_compensation: Allows increased wheel slip at low speeds for better launches.

smoothing.use_pid: PID control with P, I, D gains and D smoothing.
smoothing.change_rate: Maximum rate of change (increasing and decreasing) when PID is disabled.

multi_tune_options.target_slip_by_tune: Target slip can be varied by tune number.
multi_tune_options.launch_slip_by_tune: Launch slip varied by tune and speed.

vehicle_notes._description: Vehicle-specific information for FlashPro supported models.

civic_si_2006_2011.name: Civic Si 2006-2011
features: Full fuel, ignition, cam angle table editing
notes: 2007+ models have VSA for software traction control

civic_si_2012_2015.name: Civic Si 2012-2015
features: Full fuel, ignition, cam angle table editing

civic_si_2017.name: Civic Si 2017+
features: Ignition, fuel, idle speed table editing
Ignition and fuel temperature compensation tables
tips: Use Hondata mode + Sport torque table for different power levels via SPORT switch
If using scramble + Hondata mode, enable scramble toggle via SPORT switch
Disabling rev hang: turning cruise control main on reverts throttle to stock behavior

features._description: FlashPro hardware features and software capabilities.

hardware.connection: Connects via OBDII diagnostic port, no ECU modification necessary (except Bosch ECUs)
hardware.bluetooth: Built-in Bluetooth (V2.1 and BLE) for smartphone datalogging

software.readiness_codes: Readiness codes and smog check status
software.cam_angle_aware: Cam angle aware table editing (3D table visualization for VTC)

live_tuning.description: Real-time update of some tables without re-uploading. ECU copies tables from flash to RAM. Changes take effect in a fraction of a second.
limitations: FlashPro and laptop must be connected to vehicle
Not all tables can be live tuned
Only some tables can be live tuned simultaneously (limited RAM)
Tables reset to uploaded values on ignition cycle (FlashProManager re-uploads automatically if connected)
procedure: When finished, deselect live tuning and upload final calibration
live_tuning.tip: Only use while actively tuning. Disable for regular driving.

multi_tune.description: Switch between up to 5 tunes on the fly using cruise control switches. Tachometer shows selected tune (1000 RPM = #1 to 5000 RPM = #5).

steps: Switch off cruise control main for several seconds
Hold down cruise control resume button
Press cancel to show current tune or wait to exit

per_tune_tables: Traction control launch slip (by speed and tune)
Boost by gear (by gear, RPM, and tune)
notes: Tune number retained when vehicle off, but not through battery disconnect or reflash
Cannot change tune when cruise control main is enabled

vault.name: Hondata Vault
vault.description: Online storage for calibrations and datalogs, accessible directly from FlashProManager.
features: Search calibrations by keywords or parameters

flashpro_security.upload_protection: Optional password required before uploading calibrations (prevents unauthorized use and vehicle unlocking)

vehicle_security.lock_protection: FlashPro can only lock to a vehicle without an existing FlashPro lock

calibration_permissions.prevent_vault_upload: Do not allow upload to Vault
calibration_permissions.restrict_serial: Only allow upload to specific FlashPro serial number

tuning_guides._description: Key tuning guidance extracted from official documentation.

methods.mass_flow: Uses air flow meter (AFM/MAF) for fuel calculation
methods.speed_density: Uses manifold pressure sensor (MAP) for fuel calculation

tips: A lambda meter (wideband AFR gauge) is necessary for fuel tuning
Aim for a flat AFR plot across the RPM range
Stock wideband reads 10.7:1-20:1 but reads progressively richer as EGT increases
For forced induction, use aftermarket wideband for WOT tuning

full_load: Tune for max power, then retard until ~1 hp loss
Abort dyno run immediately if knock/pinging is detected
With forced induction, use conservative settings - engine damage occurs in seconds
ignition_tuning.part_throttle: Default calibrations usually suitable. EGT gauge helps if tuning needed.
ignition_tuning.idle: ECU uses ignition to control idle speed. Normal range: -10 to +15 degrees.
warnings: Excessive advance damages engine (bearing stress, combustion pressure)
Too little advance damages engine via excessive EGT (especially turbo)
Do not rely on knock sensor alone to prevent detonation

vtc_tuning.na_guidelines: Max cam advance after VTEC engagement to ~6500-7000 RPM, then retard back ~25 degrees to redline.
vtc_tuning.supercharged: Max cam advance (50 degrees) throughout rev range with ~10 degree retard above 7000 RPM.
vtc_tuning.turbocharged: Less advance than stock due to exhaust backpressure. Small turbos may need 0 degrees at 8000 RPM.
vtc_tuning.part_throttle: 0-5 degrees at idle, 15-30 degrees at cruise, full-load values above cruise RPM for smooth shifting.
procedure: Set VTEC high (6500+), tune low cam only
Tune fuel and ignition for each cam angle
Dyno runs at each cam angle, set angles to max power
Set VTEC low (3000) and repeat for high cam

vtec_tuning.procedure: Two dyno runs: one VTEC low (3000), one high (6500). Set VTEC point at power curve intersection.
warnings: Do not set below 2000 RPM (oil pressure too low)
Do not set above 6500 RPM (rocker arm float damage)

knock_control.formula: Ignition advance = min(MBT Ignition, MBT Ignition + knock ignition limit - (knock retard x knock control))
tuning_recommendations: Decrease MBT timing where knock limit tables are negative
For FI on pump fuel: knock limit 0 or less in boost columns
Increase knock sensitivity (reduce values) in columns 4+
K.Control starts high after flash; hold steady RPM on dyno to reduce
